name: Gluten-Free Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray an 8 x 4 inch loaf pan with nonstick spray. Coat the inside with cornmeal and set aside.
2. Dissolve sugar in warm water in a small bowl.
3. Sprinkle yeast over top. Set aside for 10 minutes or until frothy, stirring well.
4. Combine water and minute tapioca in a saucepan. Bring to a boil. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes or until thickened and clear.
5. Stir together bean flour, cornmeal, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt in a mixing bowl.
6. In a separate bowl, whisk egg whites and milk. Whisk in yeast and tapioca mixture. Stir into dry ingredients, beating until smooth.
7. Set aside for 10 minutes.
8. Turn dough out onto a surface generously dusted with cornstarch. Dust hands with cornstarch and sprinkle a little over the dough. Knead 4 to 5 times and fit into prepared pan.
9. Brush top with milk and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
10. Bake in a 400°F oven for 45 minutes or until browned and the loaf sounds hollow when tapped. Makes 1 loaf, 16 slices. Variations such as seed bread, herb bread, Foccacia & pizza crust can be added after testing.
